commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

evaluation.critique: The component has good structural hierarchy and correctly renders all data from props without hardcoding. However, it mixes design token variables with fallback hex colors (defeating the purpose of tokens), uses hardcoded pixel values (16, 24, 8px), and lacks interactive states. The forecast strip could benefit from better responsive handling for smaller screens.

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

evaluation.critique: The component has a solid grid-based layout for the periodic table, but lacks interactive polish (no hover/focus states, transitions, or active selection styling). Design token usage is inconsistent—while colors use var() properly, some spacing fallbacks use hardcoded pixel values in comments, and missing hover/active states. Data presentation is good, but the component needs visual feedback for selected elements and better keyboard accessibility.

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

evaluation.critique: The component has good data prop handling and clear hierarchy, but relies on hardcoded fallback weather data in the component defaults (Mon/Tue/Wed forecast), violating the 'never hardcode' requirement. Several style values use hardcoded pixels/colors as fallbacks instead of pure design tokens, and the card lacks interactive polish like hover states or transitions.
